skill.name: testing-patterns
skill.description: Guidance for writing Jest tests with factories, mocks, React Native test utilities, TDD workflow, and behavior-focused assertions.
skill.summary: Write maintainable Jest tests with factories, mocks, and TDD patterns.
skill.author: ChrisWiles

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, testing terminology, and npm test command examples in SKILL.md. Manual review found no executable skill code, no weak cryptography implementation, no reconnaissance behavior, and no prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown examples and fenced code snippets for Jest, React Native Testing Library, and npm test usage. They are documentation examples, not executable skill scripts, and no command injection path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visibly Markdown code examples and inline code text in a single documentation file. No script file, runtime hook, or dynamic execution mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to be false positives from words used in testing examples, such as mock, describe, and TDD content. No hash, cipher, password storage, or cryptographic API usage was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found Jest testing prose and sample data only. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to ordinary testing guidance about writing minimal code, avoiding implementation details, and running local test commands. No system probing, network scanning, host discovery, or data collection behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are headings or test methodology notes. They do not instruct the model to inspect networks, enumerate systems, or collect environment information.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent test patterns make code harder to change and review. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Jest patterns for factories, mocks, and TDD.
actual_capabilities: Explains red-green-refactor TDD workflow for unit tests.
Shows how to create reusable test factories with override support.
Provides React Native Testing Library render helper patterns.
Demonstrates Jest module mocks and GraphQL hook mocks.
Recommends behavior-focused assertions and organized describe blocks.
Lists local npm commands for running test suites and coverage.
limitations: Focuses on Jest and React Native examples, not every test framework.
Provides guidance and snippets, not a complete runnable test harness.
Does not cover end-to-end testing tools in depth.
Requires adaptation to each project structure and dependency setup.

use_cases.title: Add Unit Tests for a Component
use_cases.description: Use behavior-focused examples to write tests for rendering states, user actions, and edge cases.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Data Setup
use_cases.description: Create factory functions so teams can reuse stable default props and override only what each test needs.

use_cases.title: Coach TDD Workflow
use_cases.description: Guide developers through failing tests, minimal implementation, and refactoring after the test passes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to write a Jest test for this component. Focus on public behavior and clear test names.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Test Factory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to create a reusable factory for this data type. Include defaults and override examples.

prompt_templates.title: Mock a Dependency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to mock this module or generated hook. Keep the test focused on user-visible behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-patterns skill to review this test suite. Improve structure, factories, mocks, and TDD alignment without changing behavior.

output_examples.input: A React Native component needs tests for loading, empty, and populated states.
output_examples.output: A structured test plan with describe blocks, render helper usage, and behavior-based assertions for each state.

output_examples.input: A user object is duplicated across many Jest tests with small differences.
output_examples.output: A factory approach that defines stable defaults and lets each test override only the fields it cares about.

output_examples.input: A generated GraphQL hook must be controlled during component tests.
output_examples.output: A mocking strategy that returns loading, success, and error values while keeping assertions tied to rendered behavior.

best_practices: Write a failing test before implementing production behavior.
Use factories for props and data so tests stay consistent.
Assert user-visible behavior instead of internal implementation details.
anti_patterns: Testing that a mock was called when the real outcome is visible in the UI.
Duplicating large data objects across tests instead of using factories.
Writing broad tests that mix unrelated behaviors in one case.

faq.question: What testing framework does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Jest, with examples for React Native Testing Library and generated GraphQL hooks.

faq.question: Can this skill write complete test files?
faq.answer: It can guide complete test files, but project imports and local helpers may need adjustment.

faq.question: Does it require test-driven development?
faq.answer: No. It recommends TDD, but the patterns also help when adding tests to existing code.

faq.question: What are test factories?
faq.answer: Test factories create default props or data and allow targeted overrides for each test case.

faq.question: Does it cover end-to-end testing?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is mainly for unit and component tests.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found documentation examples only, with no executable code or malicious instructions.
